title: Colby
subtitle: Men's Soccer
call_with: Call with Assistant Coach Ben Manoogian

quick_reference.division_conference: NCAA Division III — NESCAC (New England Small College Athletic Conference)
quick_reference.head_coach: Sean Elvert (first season was 2025; hired May 2025 from Colorado College)
quick_reference.point_of_contact: Ben Manoogian, Assistant Coach (primary recruiting contact for Finn; bmanoogi@colby.edu, 207-859-4912)
quick_reference.recent_results: 2025: 5-4-6 overall, 0-4-6 NESCAC (9th of 10). Six conference draws — competitive but couldn't close games in Elvert's rebuild year.
quick_reference.engineering: No in-house engineering degree (SLAC), but two strong dual-degree pathways: 3-2 with Dartmouth's Thayer School (BA Colby + BE Dartmouth) and 3-2 with Columbia SEAS — both relevant to Finn's mechanical/aerospace interest.
quick_reference.academic_anchor: Chemistry & Biochemistry (Finn's primary major), with a brand-new science complex funded by a $150M anonymous gift (announced Sept 2025); engineering reachable via the Dartmouth/Columbia 3-2 dual-degree programs.
quick_reference.location: Waterville, Maine — rural, 714-acre campus, ~2,400 undergrads. Roughly 2,200 miles from Lafayette, CO.

where_we_stand:
This is an Ongoing Conversation with real, two-way momentum — and it is squarely a Far Reach on admissions, which should frame everything. Finn first reached out in November 2025 (then as a striker/winger) ahead of MLS NEXT Fest in Mesa, AZ. Head Coach Sean Elvert replied personally, said the staff 'enjoyed the highlight video' and would 'take in a couple halves' in Phoenix, and asked Finn to complete the recruit questionnaire — which Finn did. The relationship then deepened around Finn's position change to left wingback and a shared Colorado connection (Elvert came to Colby from Colorado College).

The most important thread is with Ben Manoogian, who has become the day-to-day contact. After Finn's May 2026 end-of-season update, Manoogian gave a substantive, encouraging reply: he congratulated Finn on qualifying for MLS NEXT Cup ('a great accomplishment... something you should be very proud of'), said 'there are some good clips in there' on the film, and — critically — laid out exactly what Colby wants from outside backs: 'aggressive outside backs who are very good in defensive 1v1's, but also able to get forward and impact the attack... two-way players who are composed on the ball and take pride in defending.' Finn smartly mirrored that profile back. Manoogian has also shared the staff's summer camp circuit (Harvard ID, Super60 in NJ, Amherst's PPA in late July) and, when Finn asked whether Harvard (July 12-13) or Colby (Aug 9) was 'better,' gave a balanced, honest answer: Harvard means ~10 institutions and more players; Colby is 'a bit more personal' with 3-4 schools and a chance to see campus and Maine.

Where we stand: warm, responsive, and genuinely tracking Finn — but no offer, no pre-read, and no stated timeline yet. This call (with Manoogian, who offered 'please feel free to reach out if you have any other questions') is the moment to move from friendly correspondence to the three things that decide everything at a NESCAC: Is Finn admissible (academic pre-read)? Is there a 2027 left wingback slot? And which camp gets him the best in-person evaluation by this staff?

part_1_background.university: Colby College is a private liberal arts college in Waterville, Maine, founded in 1813 and one of the most selective SLACs in the country (roughly a 7% acceptance rate). It enrolls about 2,400 undergraduates on a rural 714-acre campus, carries a $1.2 billion endowment, and competes in NCAA Division III as a member of the NESCAC — widely regarded as the toughest D3 conference in the nation. The motto, Lux Mentis Scientia ('Knowledge is the Light of the Mind'), signals the academic-first culture: there are no athletic scholarships, and admission for athletes runs through the same highly selective process as the general pool.
part_1_background.what_makes_distinctive: Colby pairs elite academics with a major, current investment in the sciences: in September 2025 the college announced a $150 million anonymous gift to build a new science complex. For a chemistry-focused recruit, that is a live, concrete differentiator — Finn would be arriving as Colby is expanding its STEM facilities. Colby also runs a distinctive Jan Plan (a four-week January term for a single intensive course, research, or internship) and is need-blind with strong aid, which matters given there are no athletic dollars.

academic_program.name: Department of Chemistry & Biochemistry + Dartmouth/Columbia 3-2 Dual-Degree Engineering
academic_program.description:
Chemistry is Finn's primary stated major, and Colby's Department of Chemistry & Biochemistry is a strong fit: it is an experimental, research-driven program where students do faculty-mentored projects using research-grade instrumentation, with concentrations available in biochemistry, cell and molecular biology/biochemistry, and environmental science. Recent faculty/student research has included supramolecular synthesis, superoxide detection in natural waters, and the molecular basis of cancer therapy — and the new $150M science complex will directly upgrade these facilities.

For Finn's secondary interest in mechanical/aerospace engineering, Colby (as a SLAC with no in-house engineering degree) offers two 3-2 dual-degree pathways: the long-standing program with Dartmouth's Thayer School of Engineering (graduates earn a BA from Colby plus a Bachelor of Engineering from Dartmouth, structured roughly as 2-1-1-1) and a 3-2 affiliation with Columbia's SEAS. Both require strong performance in Colby's pre-engineering math/physics/chemistry sequence (typically a 3.0-3.3+ GPA threshold) — which aligns well with Finn's AP Calc/Physics C/Chem profile. This is the realistic chemistry-to-engineering bridge at a school like Colby.
relevant_programs: Chemistry & Biochemistry — concentrations in biochemistry, cell & molecular biology/biochemistry, environmental science (faculty-mentored research, research-grade instrumentation)
New $150M science complex (announced Sept 2025) expanding STEM facilities
3-2 Dual-Degree Engineering with Dartmouth's Thayer School (BA Colby + BE Dartmouth)
Jan Plan four-week January term for research/internships

soccer_roster_academic_fit.intro: The 2025 roster is heavily stocked with players from academically rigorous prep and independent schools (Loomis Chaffee, Taft, Choate, Germantown Academy, Sidwell Friends, Horace Mann, Worcester Academy), consistent with NESCAC admissions. Colby does not list majors on its public roster, so individual majors below are not published; players are included here for hometown/profile fit relevant to Finn.

part_1_background.geographic_connection: Two Colorado anchors: defenders Charlie Scott (Jr.) and Theo Scott (So.), both from Denver and East High School, are on the roster — proof the staff already recruits and lands Colorado players. More importantly, Head Coach Sean Elvert spent 2022-2025 as an assistant at Colorado College and ran goalkeeping/college advising at Arsenal Colorado in Fort Collins, so he genuinely knows the Colorado club landscape and the players coming out of it — a connection Finn already referenced in his April 2026 email. Note the real distance, though: Waterville, ME is roughly 2,200 miles from Lafayette, CO.
part_1_background.student_life: Colby life is rural, residential, and tight-knit: nearly all students live on campus in Waterville, a small Maine city, with outdoor access (skiing, hiking) and a strong four-season, community-oriented culture. The Jan Plan term and small class sizes (about 9:1 student-faculty) mean close faculty relationships — valuable for a lab-heavy chemistry student-athlete balancing a NESCAC season. Athletes are fully integrated into the academic student body rather than siloed.
honest_reality_checks: Admissions is a Far Reach. Colby's acceptance rate is ~7% and there are no athletic scholarships — coach support helps, but Finn must be academically admissible on his own. His 3.78W/3.57UW GPA and 1380 SAT are solid but below the typical admitted profile; the fall 2026 retake (targeting 1450+) and strong AP exam scores matter a great deal here.
No pre-read has happened yet. Until Colby runs an academic pre-read, all soccer interest is provisional. This is the single most important thing to surface on the call.

The 3-2 engineering path is real but indirect. Finn would earn a Colby chemistry/physics BA first and engineering second via Dartmouth or Columbia — five years, two campuses, and contingent on hitting GPA/prerequisite thresholds. It's a great fit for his profile but is not a traditional four-year engineering degree.

Roster depth at the back is significant. The 2025 roster carries many defenders (Stewart, Sams, Kirwan, Supan, Daboo, Clifford, Robison, plus Charlie and Theo Scott), and most of the back line is underclassmen who'll still be there in 2027 — so a 2027 LWB would be competing into an established defensive group.

Distance and program stage. Waterville is ~2,200 miles from home, and the program is early in a rebuild (5-4-6 in Elvert's first year, 0-4-6 in NESCAC). That's an opportunity to help shape a new era, but it's not a finished, winning machine yet.

recent_performance: 2025 was Sean Elvert's first season as head coach, and the Mules finished 5-4-6 overall — a competitive, resilient campaign for a brand-new staff that brought in 10 first-year players.

The season opened strong: a 4-1 win over Thomas College (Jude Gussen '29 two goals; Joseph Beshel '27 PK), a 2-1 win over Southern Maine, a gritty 0-0 NESCAC draw with eventual champion-tier Tufts, a 3-1 win at Saint Joseph's, and a 2-0 win at Endicott — an encouraging early run.

The Mules showed real toughness down the stretch, closing the year on a three-game run of NESCAC draws: 1-1 at Trinity, 1-1 vs. league power Wesleyan on Senior Day, and a 1-1 rivalry draw at Bates (Santi Canencio '29 scoring late).

The honest flip side: Colby went 0-4-6 in NESCAC play, finishing 9th of 10 — six conference draws but no conference wins, and lopsided losses to Williams (0-2), Amherst (0-1), and Middlebury (0-4). The team competed but couldn't convert draws into wins in the league.
Individual note: GK Jackson Magee earned All-Region recognition (per Colby Athletics, Dec 2025), a bright spot from the rebuild year.
part_2_program.coaching_transition: Colby is in the early stage of a coaching transition. Sean Elvert was named head coach in May 2025 and just completed his first season (2025). He has brought, per the student paper, 'new ideas and fresh enthusiasm,' emphasizing development, discipline, and team chemistry while integrating a large first-year class. For a 2027 recruit, this matters: Finn would be recruited and developed entirely within Elvert and Manoogian's system, and there's a genuine 'help build the new era' angle Finn himself has already voiced ('I'd love to be part of a new chapter in Waterville').
coaching_staff: Sean Elvert — Head Coach (since May 2025). Former D3 goalkeeper at Loras College; came to Colby from Colorado College (assistant, 2022-2025) and ran goalkeeping/college advising at Arsenal Colorado in Fort Collins. Strong Colorado ties.

Ben Manoogian — Assistant Coach (since 2023-24), Finn's primary contact. Former Conn. College outside back and two-time captain, two-time First Team All-New England/All-NESCAC, Second Team Scholar All-American. Semi-pro experience (Boston City FC, plus stops in Costa Rica and Sweden).
Yuri Nascimento — Goalkeeper Coach. Former professional GK from São Paulo, Brazil (played for Cruzeiro and São Caetano); now GK director at The Pitch in Warren, Maine.
part_2_program.roster_shape: The 2025 roster is large (33 players) and skews young after a 10-player first-year intake. It's built heavily from elite prep/independent schools across the Northeast, mid-Atlantic, and beyond (NJ, PA, MA, NY, DC), with international players from Colombia, Panama, England, the DR, and a notable pair from Denver. Several key contributors are juniors who will be seniors in Finn's recruiting cycle (Jacob Sams '27, Charlie Scott '27, Joseph Beshel '27), but the bulk of the back line and the attack is underclassmen who'll still be on campus in 2027.
where_they_recruit_from: Elite Northeastern/mid-Atlantic prep & independent schools (Loomis Chaffee, Taft, Choate, Worcester Academy, Germantown Academy, Sidwell Friends, Horace Mann, South Kent)
Northeast public/state powerhouses (State College Area PA, Belmont/Bedford MA, Brookline MA)
International pipelines (Colombia, Panama, England, Dominican Republic)
Colorado — proven via Charlie & Theo Scott (Denver/East HS) and Elvert's Colorado College/Arsenal Colorado background
The summer ID-camp circuit the staff works: Harvard ID camps, Super60 (NJ), Amherst's PPA (late July), and Colby's own Aug 9 ID clinic
part_2_program.position_depth: This is the most important roster question for Finn. The 2025 roster lists a deep defensive group — Ryan Stewart (Sr.), Jacob Sams (Jr.), Andrew Kirwan (Jr.), CJ Supan (So.), Oliver Daboo (Jr.), Oliver Clifford (Fr.), Leo Robison (Fr.), Braden Jondro (Sr.), plus Denver pair Charlie Scott (Jr.) and Theo Scott (So.). Stewart and Jondro (seniors) graduate before 2027, opening spots, but much of the back line is underclassmen who remain. Crucially, Colby's listed positions are generic 'D' — they don't distinguish a dedicated left wingback, and Manoogian explicitly said personnel dictates formation while the 'aggressive, two-way outside back' principle stays constant. So the real question isn't whether they play a left wingback by name — it's whether they see Finn's two-way, attacking left-sided profile filling a specific 2027 need.

quick_facts.hometown: Not publicly listed; played his college soccer at Connecticut College (NESCAC)
quick_facts.playing_position: Outside back (defender) — two-year team captain at Conn. College
quick_facts.latest_credential: Two-time First Team All-New England & All-NESCAC; 2018 Second Team Scholar All-American; semi-pro with USL2 Boston City FC (2022)

part_3_coach.intro: Ben Manoogian is Finn's primary recruiting contact and is exactly the right coach to talk wingback play with — he played the position himself at a high level. He's a former Connecticut College outside back and two-time captain who earned two-time First Team All-New England/All-NESCAC honors and Second Team Scholar All-American recognition, then played semi-professionally (USL2 Boston City FC in 2022, plus stints in Costa Rica and Sweden) before getting into coaching. He came to Colby in 2023-24 after an assistant year at Colby-Sawyer, and his emails to Finn have been detailed, encouraging, and notably honest.
playing_career: Four-year student-athlete and two-year team captain at Connecticut College (a fellow NESCAC school)
Two-time First Team All-New England and All-NESCAC honoree
Earned Second Team Scholar All-American honors in 2018 (athletic + academic recognition)
Played outside back — the exact position group Finn is being recruited into
Semi-professional experience: USL2 Boston City FC (2022), plus stops with Soccerviza FC (Costa Rica) and Grythyttans IF (Sweden)
coaching_path: Assistant Men's Soccer Coach at Colby-Sawyer College (2022-23 season)
Joined Colby College men's soccer staff for the 2023-24 academic year as Assistant Coach
Now the primary day-to-day recruiting contact for the 2027 class; works the summer ID circuit (Harvard, Super60 NJ, Amherst PPA, Colby's own camp)
education_credentials: Bachelor of Arts in Economics, Connecticut College
NESCAC playing and academic background — understands the conference's combined athletic/academic bar firsthand
how_to_connect: He played outside back and was a two-time All-NESCAC captain at the position. Finn should talk wingback in his language — defensive 1v1 detail, recovery runs, when to commit forward — because Manoogian evaluates that role from the inside, not as a theory.

He already defined exactly what he wants: 'aggressive outside backs who are very good in defensive 1v1's, but also able to get forward and impact the attack... two-way players who are composed on the ball and take pride in defending.' Finn should anchor to that language and give concrete evidence (started every MLS NEXT game at LWB, 3G/2A while leading the defensive side).

He's a Scholar All-American who earned his degree in Economics at a NESCAC — he respects the academic-athlete combination. Finn's rigorous AP load, chemistry focus, and NHS standing are genuine rapport points, not just box-checking.

He's been honest and player-first in writing (the candid Harvard-vs-Colby camp comparison; congratulating Finn on Cup qualification). Match that tone — ask direct, mature questions and he'll give direct answers.

He came up through the NESCAC himself and is now helping build Colby's new era under Elvert. Finn's stated desire to 'be part of a new chapter in Waterville' lands authentically with a coach early in his own tenure helping shape the program.

part_4_questions.intro: Aim for 9-11 questions across five categories, in this order: open with Academics & Fit (the gating issue at a 7%-admit NESCAC), then Roster & Playing Time, then Formation & Fit (Manoogian's wheelhouse), then Development, and close with Visit & Next Steps to lock in a camp. Lead with the pre-read question — at Colby, admissibility decides everything else.

questions.question: When does Colby run academic pre-reads, and what would you need from me — transcript, test scores, AP results — to start one this summer?
questions.why_it_matters: At a ~7% acceptance rate with no athletic scholarships, a pre-read is the single gate that determines whether soccer interest can become an offer. Surfacing the timeline now tells Finn whether to push his fall SAT retake and July AP scores into the process immediately. Without a pre-read, everything else is provisional.

questions.question: My SAT is 1380 and I'm retaking in fall 2026 targeting 1450+, with AP Chem, Calc AB, and US History scores coming in July. Realistically, where does my profile sit for a supported recruit at Colby?
questions.why_it_matters: This asks Manoogian to calibrate Finn honestly against Colby's admitted-athlete band, which is candid feedback he's shown he'll give. It also signals self-awareness and a plan, and tells Finn how much the fall retake actually moves the needle for coach support.

questions.question: I'm primarily a chemistry student with a strong interest in the engineering side — how have your players used Colby's chemistry program and the 3-2 dual-degree paths with Dartmouth and Columbia alongside a full NESCAC season?
questions.why_it_matters: This ties Finn's exact academic identity to Colby's real offerings and shows he's researched the program seriously, not generically. It also tests the practical reality of pairing a lab-heavy chemistry/pre-engineering load with in-season soccer — critical for a five-year 3-2 path.

questions.question: Are you actively recruiting a left wingback / left-sided outside back in the 2027 class, or is that slot already a focus you've filled?
questions.why_it_matters: This is the direct filter that tells Finn whether there's genuine positional need. Colby's roster lists generic 'D' and carries a deep, young back line, so Finn needs to know if his specific profile fills a 2027 gap or whether he'd be depth behind returning underclassmen.

questions.question: Your back line has a lot of underclassmen returning, with Ryan Stewart and Braden Jondro graduating before 2027 — how do you see the left side of your defense evolving over the next two recruiting classes?
questions.why_it_matters: Referencing the actual roster shows Manoogian that Finn has done his homework and is thinking like a competitor. The answer reveals the real depth chart Finn would enter and whether the graduating seniors open a genuine path on the left.

questions.question: What's your timeline for 2027 offers — are you a staff that commits juniors early, or do you tend to wait until senior fall to evaluate more?
questions.why_it_matters: This surfaces how aggressively Colby recruits and when Finn needs to have his pre-read and camp evaluation done to be in the conversation. It helps Finn triage Colby against other targets and avoid missing a window if the staff moves early.

questions.question: You described wanting two-way outside backs who win defensive 1v1s but also impact the attack — when you watch my film, what specifically do you want to see more of to be convinced I fit that mold at the NESCAC level?
questions.why_it_matters: This directly references Manoogian's own words from his May 20 email, turning his stated criteria into actionable feedback. It invites concrete coaching input Finn can train toward this summer, and it shows he listened closely and takes the standard seriously.

questions.question: You mentioned personnel dictates the formation but your principles stay constant — in a given season, how much does your left-sided defender get to push into the attack versus sit in a back four?
questions.why_it_matters: Finn transitioned from striker and his attacking instincts are a selling point, so he needs to know how much license the role gives going forward. The answer tells him whether his attacking profile is an asset or something he'd have to rein in within Colby's system.

questions.question: As a coach who played outside back and captained at the NESCAC level, what's the biggest adjustment club wingbacks have to make defensively when they get to your league?
questions.why_it_matters: This leans into Manoogian's playing background and invites the kind of insider development insight only a former outside back can give. It also frames Finn as a player focused on the defensive side of the role — exactly the 'takes pride in defending' trait Manoogian said he wants.

questions.question: What does a successful 2027 recruit look like to you and Coach Elvert as you build this new era at Colby — position need, player type, academic profile, or all three?
questions.why_it_matters: This open-ended question lets the staff define their bar without Finn anchoring it, revealing what they're truly optimizing for in a rebuild year. Given Elvert is only one season in, the answer also shows how recruiting fits their longer-term vision for the program.

questions.question: Between the Harvard camp on July 12-13 and the Colby ID clinic on August 9, which gives you and Coach Elvert the best look at me specifically — and would attending Colby's camp also be the right time for a campus visit and to meet the chemistry side?
questions.why_it_matters: Manoogian framed these as 'different opportunities' rather than ranking them, so this presses gently for which event maximizes evaluation by the Colby staff specifically. Pairing the Colby camp with a campus/academic visit also advances both the soccer and the all-important admissions/fit pieces in one trip.

closers: Reaffirm genuine interest and the 'new chapter in Waterville' fit: Finn wants to help build what Elvert and Manoogian are creating, and the two-way outside-back profile Manoogian described is exactly how Finn sees his own game.

Lock the next concrete step: confirm which camp (Harvard July 12-13 or Colby Aug 9) Finn will attend, tell Manoogian he'll register and confirm the date, and ask the staff to evaluate him in person there.

Tee up the academic pieces: let Manoogian know the fall SAT retake (targeting 1450+) and July AP scores are coming, and ask him to flag what's needed to start a pre-read so admissibility moves forward in parallel.

Offer to keep him posted on the summer: Finn is starting at left wingback for Flatirons FC USL Academy this summer — promise periodic film/progress updates, which Manoogian explicitly invited ('we look forward to continuing to track your progress').
